parsed_review.summary: CP00-792 is a descriptor-only RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening "service implementation" pack (risk B, 40 units, RP26.P02.M03.S01-RP26.P02.M04.S10) and its pack-level claim is descriptor-level verification only — it does NOT claim runtime production readiness or enterprise trust. Baseline integrity verified: pack_id/risk/unit_count/range/program match across manifest, review-baseline, registry binding (registry.js:120), contract latest_pack/latest_projection, live plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:36), and next-pack-queue (live_latest_pack_id CP00-792, next RP26.P02.M04.S11). Deliverable distribution (contract2/implementation20/security_audit4/ui4/claude_review2/failure_recovery2/test4/fixture1/hermes_evidence1=40) and micro-title split (Primary 30 / Secondary 10) match ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP792_REQUIREMENTS and a manual count of the 40 manifest units. Implementation (service.js:403 createEnterpriseSaasCp792ServiceImplementationDescriptor) extends the CP791 source descriptor following the established pattern, with SERVICE_IMPLEMENTATION_ROWS (30) for M03 and SERVICE_ROWS.slice(0,10) for M04, unique row keys, per-row runtime-closed flags in ROW_EXTRAS (executes_api_handler/persists_idempotency_key/acquires_runtime_lock/creates_database_rows all false), and the comprehensive frozen ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION applied to every result. validators.js enforces descriptor-only, runtime_execution=false, no tenant/identity/directory/secret payloads, no cross-tenant route, no key/secret material, no permission-decision detail, no audit-event body, no real tenant data, no enterprise-trust claim, plus SSO/SCIM descriptor validation, Hermes/Claude gate, and handoff drift checks. Tests: npm test reports # tests 3061 / # pass 3061 / # fail 0 (exit 0), including CP792-specific assertions that the descriptor stays runtime-closed, no API execution, no idempotency/lock persistence, Claude read-only & non-final, handoff to CP00-793, and contract promotion. Validation: all 14 command-evidence commands exit 0 (closeout-pack-plan, closeout-pack, test, validate, spec:requirements, weighted, fullplan, goal:closeout, rp00:control-plane, critical-rps, rp26, rp26:enterprise-saas, node --check, git diff --check); rp26:enterprise-saas:validate passes; contract validation marker valid:true/errors:[]. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false (expected pre-review state), no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; command-evidence attests no real client/matter/document/billing/credential/secret data. Authority boundary preserved: Claude is read_only independent reviewer and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human final approval required for runtime opening; Hermes control-plane patterns are confined to descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write) and are not embedded in product runtime. CP178+ review hardening: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1226-byte valid JSON), and no prior review attempt is being passed off as valid evidence (valid_review_receipts/invalid_review_attempts empty; the pending 0-byte raw-output is this in-flight review's placeholder). The two tracked-modified control-room files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html) are explicitly listed as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved in changed-file-scope and the manifest worktree_transition_policy and are correctly not treated as pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found; nothing blocks this CP's closeout goal.
